patterns.name: Anonymous effect
patterns.mechanism: Absence of rating lowers perceived threat → reduced attention
patterns.mitigation: Assign imaginary 2700 rating to every anonymous opponent

patterns.name: Automatic grab
patterns.mitigation: 3-second pause + 'A CO ON?' before every capture

patterns.name: Attention tunneling
patterns.mechanism: Local tactical stimulus overrides global board evaluation
patterns.it_analogy: Fixing one bug while creating another
patterns.mitigation: Set 15-min timer; ask: 'Has a new problem emerged elsewhere?'

patterns.name: Controlled vs. uncontrolled risk
patterns.mechanism: Difference lies in conscious calculation before action
patterns.mitigation: Every sacrifice must have predefined exit condition

patterns.name: Metacognitive activation
patterns.mechanism: Actively asking 'What is my opponent threatening?'
patterns.mitigation: In-game checklist before every critical move

patterns.name: Resilience after mistake
patterns.mechanism: Blunder does not cascade; player recovers composure

patterns.name: Color as modulator
patterns.mechanism: As Black patient and reactive; as White impulsive and proactive
patterns.mitigation: 'Play White as if Black' — imagine being down a pawn

patterns.name: Systematic strangulation
patterns.mechanism: Accumulate small advantages, convert in endgame without sacrifices
patterns.mitigation: Reinforce — this is a core strength

patterns.name: Poison pawn / Bait trap
patterns.mechanism: Leave a pawn/figure seemingly hanging to punish opponent's automatic grab
patterns.mitigation: Continue developing — this is the strongest weapon

patterns.name: Impulsive check block
patterns.mechanism: Blocking a check with a piece without calculating king safety or material loss
patterns.it_analogy: Silencing an alert instead of fixing the root cause
patterns.mitigation: When in check, evaluate king moves before considering blocks

patterns.name: Passive-active role confusion
patterns.mechanism: Playing passively when activity is required (or vice versa), often due to misjudging the position's dynamic potential
patterns.it_analogy: Using a synchronous call where async is needed — blocking the event loop
patterns.mitigation: Before each move, classify position as 'tactical' or 'positional' and adjust

patterns.name: Early queen exchange for technical win
patterns.mechanism: When opponent's king cannot castle, exchange queens to exploit king's vulnerability with active pieces
patterns.it_analogy: Disabling a service's API gateway to expose internal endpoints

patterns.name: Provocation sacrifice for open file
patterns.mechanism: Offer a harmless-looking exchange that opens a file toward opponent's king while retaining central control
patterns.it_analogy: Intentionally triggering a controlled failover to test disaster recovery

patterns.name: X-ray pin exploitation
patterns.mechanism: Aligning rook/queen with opponent's queen on an open file, then removing the shielding piece
patterns.it_analogy: Exploiting a misconfigured CORS policy to access internal data

patterns.name: Repetition avoidance greed
patterns.mechanism: Refusing a clear threefold repetition because 'I have a better position' or 'I don't want a draw', leading to unnecessary risk and collapse
patterns.it_analogy: Refusing to merge a stable PR because 'I can ship one more feature' — ends up breaking production
patterns.mitigation: Before refusing repetition: 5-second pause + 'Is it really better than draw?'

patterns.name: Visual pattern misrecognition (forced move illusion)
patterns.mechanism: Mistaking a threatening-looking piece placement for a forcing sequence, without calculating actual replies
patterns.it_analogy: Assuming a function is idempotent because its name contains 'safe' — without reading the implementation
patterns.mitigation: Before 'forcing' moves: ask 'Is this actually forcing, or a visual illusion?'

patterns.name: Active defense under material deficit
patterns.mechanism: When down material, avoid exchanges, keep pieces active, create threats against opponent's king, and wait for opponent's time trouble or blunder
patterns.it_analogy: Server under DDoS — instead of shutting down, return fake 200 OKs and slow down the attacker
patterns.mitigation: Reinforce — this is a core strength

patterns.name: Desperate Gambit Mode
patterns.mechanism: When objectively lost (eval < -3), reject simplifying exchanges, actively seek tactical chaos on the opponent's king side, and wait for automatic grab from opponent
patterns.it_analogy: When server is crashing, instead of graceful shutdown, start random port scans to confuse the attacker
patterns.mitigation: Reinforce — but only as last resort. Primary goal: prevent lost positions

rituals.name: 2 seconds and one question
rituals.description: Before every capture: 'What about him?' (What is opponent's reply?)

rituals.name: Repetition = 5-second pause + A CO ON?
rituals.description: Before refusing threefold repetition: 'Is it really better than draw?'

rituals.name: Anonymous = Magnus Carlsen (ELO 2700)
rituals.description: Assign imaginary high rating to every Anonymous opponent

rituals.name: Play White as if Black
rituals.description: Imagine you are down a pawn as White to compensate for impulsivity

rituals.name: 5-second pause after mistake
rituals.description: Deep breath before next move to prevent cascade

rituals.name: Check = King safety first
rituals.description: When in check, evaluate king moves before considering blocks
